chief_complaint: A 28-year-old woman presents with jaundice, chest discomfort, and a history of chronic diarrhea, weight loss, tremors, and exertional dyspnea.
history: The patient has a history of thyrotoxicosis and has been on propylthiouracil and propranolol for two weeks. She also reports chronic diarrhea, weight loss, tremors, and exertional dyspnea.
exam: Physical examination revealed jaundice, proptosis, a large goiter, and tremors.
labs: Laboratory tests indicated elevated liver enzymes, hypokalemia, and markedly elevated thyroid hormones. ECG revealed total AV block.
